query: I explicitly want to run $powerset login on a fresh MacBook with my @powerset.co Google account. Refresh everything.
expected_behavior: The legacy explicit-login alias still walks the full first-run sequence: gcloud auth check for the @powerset.co account, Auth0 PKCE login via the auth primitive, GCP secret provisioning/env pull, MCP install, and a final doctor check. It must never offer to skip consent, fabricate credentials, or ask for a password in chat.

query: I have a personal @gmail.com account, no @powerset.co. What can I still set up?
expected_behavior: The plan explains the non-Powerset path honestly: OSS-friendly skills work with the user's own API keys (e.g. their own OpenRouter/OpenAI key), and per-user GCP Secret Manager env pull only works if a maintainer has explicitly provisioned that email. It must not fabricate credentials or ask the user to paste passwords.

query: I think I already ran powerset-login a while back. Can you verify everything is still healthy?
expected_behavior: Verification only: run the doctor health check, auth whoami, and MCP status. The plan must NOT proactively re-run a fresh login or re-pull secrets when the existing state is healthy — verification and re-provisioning are different operations.

query: My MCP suddenly stopped working. claude mcp list says powerset-search failed to connect.
expected_behavior: Diagnose before destroying: the most likely cause is Auth0 access-token expiry (~24h), so the plan refreshes the bearer token via the auth primitive's token path (which auto-refreshes from the stored refresh token) and reinstalls/refreshes the MCP registration. It must not delete credentials, uninstall things, or restart from a full login as the first move.
